elements.text:
Progress since 2018
COVID-19 has led to the establishment of initiatives with a focus on open technology approaches.
Growing recognition that open IP is insufficient and open know-how is critical for complex technologies - a fact long recongised in open hardware.
Largest explosion of open hardware projects in history during 2020 - still learning from how they went, how open they really were, collaboration dynamics, impact. 
Opportunities
Policy alignment between open science hardware goals and the broader goal of equitable access to hardware - timely issue for policy makers.
Funders are exploring this space to a greater extent.
Broken supply chains during COVID-19 have turned a policy spotlight on equitable access to hardware (particularly biomedical hardware)
